target.name: RoboTwin
target.founder: Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, David Polák

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RoboTwin is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Megi Mejdrachová, Ladislav Dvořák, and David Polák. The company has raised €2.3M in EIC funding to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine private robotics companies (1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics among the top tier) and one public company (277810.KQ).

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. Within the eleven same-sector peers identified in Robotics & Manufacturing, no subset of three or more companies met simultaneous filters for seed stage, adjacent funding rounds, or similar valuation tier. This constraint reflects the fragmentation of the robotics sector and the relative sparsity of disclosed seed-stage valuations. Consequently, traditional comparable-company triangulation could not be reliably executed with the available data.

Given the methodological limitations, a credible valuation range cannot be derived from the provided dataset. The analysis exposes a material gap: while a relevant peer universe exists in the robotics space, the heterogeneity of business models, deployment stages, and capital structures between seed-stage and growth-stage robotics companies creates significant comparability risk. Additional data on peer valuations, revenue multiples (where applicable), or venture capital entry terms would be required to support a triangulated valuation for RoboTwin.
